analyst_summary: For SPY, the aggregate options surface leans bullish — more call-heavy OI and flow. That reflects overall market positioning in the chain, not a specific actor; heavy clusters can sometimes line up with large books, but we do not label institutions. Model confidence is 52% for the next 1-2 weeks horizon.

signals.name: Call Open Interest Share
signals.explanation: A larger share of call OI points to aggregate upside interest in the chain — sometimes consistent with sizable call interest, but not exclusive to any one player.

signals.name: Put/Call OI Ratio
signals.explanation: Elevated put/call OI can indicate hedging pressure or defensive positioning.

signals.name: Intraday Options Flow (Calls Share)
signals.explanation: Call-heavy volume relative to puts may indicate bullish tactical flow.

signals.name: Gamma Tilt
signals.explanation: Positive gamma tilt can support stabilization and upside continuation.

positioning.name: Call Open Interest Share
positioning.explanation: A larger share of call OI points to aggregate upside interest in the chain — sometimes consistent with sizable call interest, but not exclusive to any one player.

positioning.name: Put/Call OI Ratio
positioning.explanation: Elevated put/call OI can indicate hedging pressure or defensive positioning.

positioning.name: Intraday Options Flow (Calls Share)
positioning.explanation: Call-heavy volume relative to puts may indicate bullish tactical flow.

positioning.name: Gamma Tilt
positioning.explanation: Positive gamma tilt can support stabilization and upside continuation.

volatility.name: ATM Implied Volatility · IV Rank
volatility.explanation: ATM IV is 18.00% (percentage points). Intra-chain IV rank 33% — how that ATM print ranks versus all implied volatilities in this snapshot.

volatility.name: 25-Delta Skew
volatility.explanation: IV of the ~25-delta put minus the ~25-delta call (nearest expiry). Positive skew often reflects downside protection demand.

volatility.name: Term Structure
volatility.explanation: Need two expirations with ATM IV to infer term structure.

flow.name: Unusual Activity Score
flow.explanation: Counts strikes where volume is elevated versus open interest (ratio > 2, volume > 500), scaled into a 0–100-style intensity score.

flow.name: Aggregate Volume / OI
flow.explanation: Total options volume divided by total open interest — higher values can indicate more tactical trading versus stuck inventory.

gamma.name: Net Gamma Exposure
gamma.explanation: Sum of gamma × OI × 100 × spot for calls minus puts — a coarse dealer-gamma proxy.

gamma.name: Gamma Regime
gamma.explanation: Net gamma is positive — dealers are more likely to stabilize moves (fade extremes) if this positioning persists.

structure.name: Max Pain Strike
structure.explanation: Strike that minimizes total intrinsic value paid to option holders at expiration (nearest expiry only) — a common magnet narrative, not a forecast.

structure.name: Strike Magnets (OI)
structure.explanation: Largest open-interest strikes in the nearest expiration — where inventory tends to cluster.

structure.name: Implied Move (Straddle)
structure.explanation: ATM straddle mid / spot for the nearest expiration — a rough implied one-standard-deviation range proxy.

regime_explanation: Net gamma is positive — dealers are more likely to stabilize moves (fade extremes) if this positioning persists.

scenarios.label: Bullish continuation
scenarios.narrative: Upside continuation if SPY holds above option-supported levels.

scenarios.label: Bearish unwind
scenarios.narrative: Downside pressure if hedging demand rises and calls unwind in SPY.

scenarios.label: Range-bound
scenarios.narrative: Mean-reverting regime likely if neither side gains flow dominance.
